decisions.title: Prioritize API Performance vs New Features for Q1
decisions.context: Multiple API latency issues surfaced (GetPhaseAnnotations, BatchResolveCalculatedChannels, GetStructuredData, GetAnnotations). Need to decide whether to dedicate sprint capacity to performance or continue feature velocity.

arguments.for: API latency directly impacts Decisions Captured metric - slow systems reduce user adoption. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar.
arguments.against: Python Rules, Canvas, and Family features are customer-committed Q1 deliverables. Performance work could slip roadmap items.

decisions.title: Commit to Fresh Data Reads architecture for Sift Edge
decisions.context: Two parallel tickets on Fresh Data Reads (MemTable ENG-8228, Local Catalog ENG-8234) suggest architectural foundation work for edge deployment. Need decision on timeline and resource allocation.

arguments.for: Critical for 'Build the Moat' - edge capabilities differentiate from competition. Enables offline decision capture.
arguments.against: Core platform stability issues (API latency, memory usage) may need attention first. Edge has smaller immediate customer base.

decisions.title: Scope Family Rule Execution for V1 Launch
decisions.context: Family-related tickets span backend stats (ENG-7954), frontend kickoff (ENG-7960), config defaults (ENG-8777), rule creation (ENG-8256), and polish (ENG-8952, ENG-8953). Need to define V1 scope vs future iterations.
decisions.blockedBy: Customer validation sessions not scheduled until 03-15

arguments.for: Family Rules enable reusable decision logic across assets - core to 'Embed Deeper' pillar and Python Rules product success.
arguments.against: Feature complexity high. Incremental release (rules first, families later) might deliver value faster with less risk.

decisions.title: Invest in Export Memory Optimization vs Defer to Q2
decisions.context: ENG-8299 addresses Export memory usage. Exports critical for customer workflows but optimization work competes with new feature development.

arguments.for: Export failures block decision workflows. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' - core functionality must be reliable at scale.
arguments.against: Workarounds exist (smaller exports, pagination). Resources might be better spent on Canvas or Ask SIFT which drive new customer value.

decisions.title: Standardize on sift_client vs maintain dual libraries
decisions.context: ENG-8910 migrates sift_py modules to sift_client. Need decision on deprecation timeline and customer migration support.

arguments.for: Single client library reduces maintenance burden, improves docs, easier for customers. Supports 'Embed Deeper' through better dev experience.
arguments.against: Migration breaks existing customer code. Need to provide migration path, documentation, and support which diverts resources.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of week) leaving minimal focus time for strategic decisions. Three open decisions require deep analysis but calendar fragmented.
alerts: 5 decisions pending with deadlines in next 2 weeks - schedule decision-making sessions
API performance review scheduled for Thu 3/6 lacks prep time - consider rescheduling
Customer validation for Family Rules blocked until 3/15 but decision due 3/10 - timeline misalignment
